title: Bulgarian Present Tense
description: Learn how to form and use the present tense in Bulgarian with patterns, examples, and a few quick checks.

content.title: What Present Tense Does
content.description: Use the present tense for actions happening now, repeated actions, and general facts.

content.title: Important idea
content.text: Bulgarian present tense can often cover both 'I read' and 'I am reading' depending on context.

content.note: The same form can also mean 'I read' in a general sense.
This is a repeated action, so the present tense is perfect here.

content.title: Present tense toolkit
content.description: Move between pronouns, endings, verb forms, and practice without scrolling away from the main lesson.

tabs.label: Pronouns
tabs.description: The subject pronouns are simple, but they matter when you practice endings.

content.title: Pronoun shortcut
content.text: If you can spot the pronoun quickly, the verb ending usually becomes much easier to predict.

tabs.label: Endings
tabs.description: These broad patterns show up in many verbs.

content.title: Endings to remember
content.note: The exact stem depends on the verb, so always learn the full form with examples.

tabs.label: Verb forms
tabs.description: Two common verbs help show how the endings change in real sentences.

content.note: A small time phrase makes the present tense feel more natural.

content.bg: Той работи тук и говори английски.
content.en: He works here and speaks English.
content.note: A single sentence can show more than one present-tense form at once.

tabs.label: Practice
tabs.description: Short sentences are the fastest way to make the pattern stick.

content.question: Which pronoun usually matches the ending '-ем / -им'?
content.note: Once you know the pronoun, the ending is much easier to predict.

content.title: More Present Tense Practice
content.description: Use these extra examples after the tabbed toolkit to keep the lesson moving.

content.note: A simple action sentence that fits the same present-tense pattern.
The adverb helps show how the present tense works in a full sentence.

content.question: Which sentence shows a routine action?
content.note: Look for the everyday or repeated meaning rather than only the translation.
